name: Rainbow Drive-In
tagline: The plate lunch cathedral since 1961 — mixed plate with gravy is the move.
mustOrder: mixed plate with gravy ($12–14) — teriyaki beef + mahimahi + fried chicken, 2 scoops rice, mac salad
parking: Small lot in front, fills up fast at lunch — street parking on Kanaina Ave
talkStory: Founded in 1961 by Seiju Ifuku, a WWII veteran of the legendary 442nd Regimental Combat Team — the most decorated unit in US military history, composed entirely of Japanese-Americans from Hawaii. 'Plate lunch' evolved from plantation-era bento boxes; Rainbow helped codify the modern two-scoop-rice format. Barack Obama ate here regularly growing up and during presidential visits. The mixed plate with gravy over rice is the canonical order. Take your plate one block to Kapiolani Park and eat under a monkeypod tree facing Diamond Head.
